title: Biotech news from around the world - Nature Biotechnology
text:

Scientists from Stellenbosch University and the Agricultural Research Council develop Africa’s first gene-edited grapevine. Using CRISPR–Cas9 technology to silence the VvDMR6.1 gene reduced the grapevine’s vulnerability to downy mildew, a fungal disease, and also increased the plants’ ability to conserve water, simultaneously helping their defenses against both biological pests and environmental hardships.The GenomeIndia genomic sequencing project identifies 130 million genetic variants in the population, almost a third of which have never been reported in global scientific databases. The dataset, funded by India’s Department of Biotechnology, analyzed the genomes of 9,768 healthy people from 83 populations to reveal genetic risk factors in some populations, including gene variants that affect how the body processes certain drugs, variants linked to anesthesia-related complications, and high levels of genetic homozygosity, which can be a risk factor for recessive genetic diseases.
